title: Benzylisoquinoline

infobox.value: structural [isomers](/wiki/Isomer "Isomer"). The 1-benzylisoquinoline [moiety](/wiki/Moiety_(chemistry) "Moiety (chemistry)") can be identified within numerous compounds of pharmaceutical interest, such as [moxaverine](/wiki/Moxaverine "Moxaverine"); but most notably it is found within the structures of a wide variety of plant [natural products](/wiki/Natural_product "Natural product"), collectively referred to as [benzylisoquinoline alkaloids](/wiki/Benzylisoquinoline_alkaloids "Benzylisoquinoline alkaloids"). This class is exemplified in part by the following compounds: [papaverine](/wiki/Papaverine "Papaverine"), [noscapine](/wiki/Noscapine "Noscapine"), [codeine](/wiki/Codeine "Codeine"), [morphine](/wiki/Morphine "Morphine"), [apomorphine](/wiki/Apomorphine "Apomorphine"), [berberine](/wiki/Berberine "Berberine"), [tubocurarine](/wiki/Tubocurarine "Tubocurarine").

Hagel, Jillian M; Morris, Jeremy S; Lee, Eun-Jeong; Desgagne-Penix, Isabel; Bross, Crystal D; Chang, Limei; Chen, Xue; Farrow, Scott C; Zhang, Ye (2015). ["Transcriptome analysis of 20 taxonomically related benzylisoquinoline alkaloid-producing plants"](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4575454). *BMC Plant Biol*. **15**: 227. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1186/s12870-015-0596-0](https://doi.org/10.1186%2Fs12870-015-0596-0). [PMC](/wiki/PMC_(identifier) "PMC (identifier)") [4575454](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4575454). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [26384972](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26384972).

Hagel, Jillian M; Facchini, Peter J (2013). ["Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid metabolism: A century of discovery and a brave new world"](https://doi.org/10.1093%2Fpcp%2Fpct020). *Plant Cell Physiol*. **54** (5): 647–672. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1093/pcp/pct020](https://doi.org/10.1093%2Fpcp%2Fpct020). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [23385146](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23385146).

Battersby, A. R.; Binks, R.; Francis, R. J.; McCaldin, D. J.; Ramuz, H. (1964). "Alkaloid biosynthesis> Part IV. 1-Benzylisoquinolines as precursors of thebaine, codeine, and morphine". *J Chem Soc*: 3600.

first_paragraph: Substitution of the [heterocycle](/wiki/Heterocyclic_compound "Heterocyclic compound") [isoquinoline](/wiki/Isoquinoline "Isoquinoline") at the C1 position by a [benzyl group](/wiki/Benzyl_group "Benzyl group") provides 1‑benzylisoquinoline, the most widely examined of the numerous **benzylisoquinoline** structural [isomers](/wiki/Isomer "Isomer"). The 1-benzylisoquinoline [moiety](/wiki/Moiety_(chemistry) "Moiety (chemistry)") can be identified within numerous compounds of pharmaceutical interest, such as [moxaverine](/wiki/Moxaverine "Moxaverine"); but most notably it is found within the structures of a wide variety of plant [natural products](/wiki/Natural_product "Natural product"), collectively referred to as [benzylisoquinoline alkaloids](/wiki/Benzylisoquinoline_alkaloids "Benzylisoquinoline alkaloids"). This class is exemplified in part by the following compounds: [papaverine](/wiki/Papaverine "Papaverine"), [noscapine](/wiki/Noscapine "Noscapine"), [codeine](/wiki/Codeine "Codeine"), [morphine](/wiki/Morphine "Morphine"), [apomorphine](/wiki/Apomorphine "Apomorphine"), [berberine](/wiki/Berberine "Berberine"), [tubocurarine](/wiki/Tubocurarine "Tubocurarine").

Chemical compound

Substitution of the [heterocycle](/wiki/Heterocyclic_compound "Heterocyclic compound") [isoquinoline](/wiki/Isoquinoline "Isoquinoline") at the C1 position by a [benzyl group](/wiki/Benzyl_group "Benzyl group") provides 1‑benzylisoquinoline, the most widely examined of the numerous **benzylisoquinoline** structural [isomers](/wiki/Isomer "Isomer"). The 1-benzylisoquinoline [moiety](/wiki/Moiety_(chemistry) "Moiety (chemistry)") can be identified within numerous compounds of pharmaceutical interest, such as [moxaverine](/wiki/Moxaverine "Moxaverine"); but most notably it is found within the structures of a wide variety of plant [natural products](/wiki/Natural_product "Natural product"), collectively referred to as [benzylisoquinoline alkaloids](/wiki/Benzylisoquinoline_alkaloids "Benzylisoquinoline alkaloids"). This class is exemplified in part by the following compounds: [papaverine](/wiki/Papaverine "Papaverine"), [noscapine](/wiki/Noscapine "Noscapine"), [codeine](/wiki/Codeine "Codeine"), [morphine](/wiki/Morphine "Morphine"), [apomorphine](/wiki/Apomorphine "Apomorphine"), [berberine](/wiki/Berberine "Berberine"), [tubocurarine](/wiki/Tubocurarine "Tubocurarine").

## Biosynthesis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Benzylisoquinoline&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biosynthesis")]

(*S*)-[Norcoclaurine](/wiki/Norcoclaurine "Norcoclaurine") ([higenamine](/wiki/Higenamine "Higenamine")) has been identified as the central 1-benzyl-tetrahydro-isoquinoline precursor[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) from which numerous complex biosynthetic pathways eventually emerge. These pathways collectively lead to the structurally disparate compounds comprising the broad classification of plant natural products referred to as benzylisoquinoline alkaloids (BIA), which have been comprehensively discussed by Hagel.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The biosynthesis of [(S)-norcoclaurine](/wiki/(S)-norcoclaurine "(S)-norcoclaurine"), which is catalyzed by [(*S*)-norcoclaurine synthase](/wiki/(S)-norcoclaurine_synthase "(S)-norcoclaurine synthase"), is accomplished by the stereoselective condensation of [dopamine](/wiki/Dopamine "Dopamine") and 4-hydroxyphenylacetaldehyde (4-HPAA); each of these compounds is prepared by multiple enzymatic transformations from [L-tyrosine](/wiki/L-Tyrosine "L-Tyrosine").

It is of interest to note that early studies initially identified norlaudanosoline ([tetrahydropapaveroline](/wiki/Tetrahydropapaveroline "Tetrahydropapaveroline")) as the purported central precursor for the biosynthesis of BIAs.[[4]](#cite_note-4) However, more than two decades later it was finely unequivocally established that [(*S*)-norcoclaurine](/wiki/(S)-norcoclaurine "(S)-norcoclaurine") was the central precursor for the biosynthesis of the structurally diverse BIAs.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)
